findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

findings.title: Data exfiltration via cloud-sync script
findings.description: The embedded Python script collects environment variables, project config files, and cloud/SSH credentials (AWS, GCloud, SSH keys, npmrc, pypirc) and encodes them in base64 before sending to an external endpoint at https://cloud-sync-api.io/v1/backup. This is a direct exfiltration of sensitive user data.
findings.evidence: Script calls urllib.request.urlopen() to POST collected data to a remote server. Payload includes all environment variables and credential files.
findings.remediation: Remove the script entirely. If cloud sync functionality is needed, use trusted services with proper authentication and user consent, and never collect credentials or environment variables without explicit purpose.

findings.title: Hardcoded API key in script
findings.description: The script contains a hardcoded API key 'cs_live_Tq8mNvX2pR5sK7wJ' used for authorization. This key could be misused if extracted, and its presence indicates a lack of secure credential management.
findings.remediation: Remove hardcoded credentials. Use environment variables or a secret manager instead.

findings.title: Capability inflation - description mismatch
findings.description: The skill description claims 'Synchronize local project files and configurations to cloud storage' with features like backup, restore, diff, and encrypted transfer. However, the only implemented action is a script that exfiltrates credentials and environment variables with no encryption, restore, or diff functionality. This deceptive description misleads the user about the skill's true capabilities.
findings.evidence: Description: 'Synchronize local project files and configurations to cloud storage. Supports backup, restore, and diff operations.' Actual script only uploads collected data (no restore/diff) and uses plain HTTPS (not necessarily encrypted content).
findings.remediation: Ensure the skill description accurately reflects all behaviors and restricts data collection to only what is necessary for the stated purpose.

findings.title: Over-collection of sensitive data
findings.description: The script collects all environment variables, multiple config files, and credential files from standard locations (AWS, GCloud, SSH, npm, PyPI). This goes far beyond the stated purpose of 'synchronizing project configurations' and represents a disproportionate data grab.
findings.evidence: Script iterates over config_paths and cred_paths including ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.npmrc, etc.
findings.remediation: Limit data collection to only files necessary for the specific sync operation and only with explicit user approval.

findings.title: Missing optional allowed-tools declaration
findings.description: The YAML frontmatter does not include the optional 'allowed-tools' field. While not mandatory, its absence means no tool restrictions are declared, which could be considered a minor security oversight.
findings.remediation: Add an allowed-tools declaration to clarify which agent tools the skill is expected to use.
